text: tf_preregister() renders preregistration document single markdown string. records constructs, propositions predictions fixed order, output stable across runs suitable deposit. Passing path writes markdown disk LF line endings, convenient document committed repository uploaded registry.
code: prereg <- tf_preregister(theory) cat(prereg) #> # Preregistration: Network theory of panic disorder #>  #> - Theory ID: panic-network-2026 #> - Schema version: 1.0 #> - Maturity: developing #> - Derivation chain verified: yes #>  #> ## Hypotheses #> 1. [point] An interoceptive challenge raises arousal to a specified level within 90 seconds. (derives from: p1, p3) #> 2. [interval] Avoidance frequency falls within a 20-35% band after exposure therapy. (derives from: p2) #> 3. [directional] Higher perceived threat is associated with more avoidance. (derives from: p2) #>  #> ## Severity #> - pred1: severity 1.0, risk 0.9 #> - pred2: severity 0.7, risk 0.7 #> - pred3: severity 0.3, risk 0.4 prereg_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".md") invisible(tf_preregister(theory, path = prereg_path)) file.exists(prereg_path) #> [1] TRUE

title: Mapping the literature
text: tf_litmap() computes three things records. counts keyword co-occurrence within records, groups co-occurring keywords thematic components, counts reference co-citation. edge kept co-occurrence count reaches min_link (default 2), suppresses incidental pairings. Records read file order every result sorted, map fully deterministic. thematic components connected groups co-occurring keywords. fixture constructed threshold-two graph separates cleanly four themes. Co-citation computed way references field. Two works linked cite pair sources least min_link times.
code: lm <- tf_litmap(corpus, min_link = 2)  lm$n_records #> [1] 8 unlist(lm$keywords)                 # the vocabulary of the corpus #> [1] "appraisal"                      "arousal"                        #> [3] "avoidance"                      "catastrophic misinterpretation" #> [5] "exposure"                       "genetics"                       #> [7] "heritability"                   "interoception" lm$keyword_cooccurrence[[1]]        # first kept co-occurrence edge #> $a #> [1] "appraisal" #>  #> $b #> [1] "catastrophic misinterpretation" #>  #> $count #> [1] 2 for (theme in lm$themes) {   cat(theme$id, ":", paste(unlist(theme$keywords), collapse = ", "), "\n") } #> theme_1 : appraisal, catastrophic misinterpretation  #> theme_2 : arousal, interoception  #> theme_3 : avoidance, exposure  #> theme_4 : genetics, heritability lm$co_citation #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$a #> [1] "barlow2002" #>  #> [[1]]$b #> [1] "clark1986" #>  #> [[1]]$count #> [1] 3 #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$a #> [1] "bouton2001" #>  #> [[2]]$b #> [1] "craske2008" #>  #> [[2]]$count #> [1] 2

code: report <- tf_check(theory)  report$aggregate_score   # weighted score, 0-100, rounded to 1 dp #> [1] 77.6 report$gate              # "pass", "blocked", or "advisory" (draft maturity) #> [1] "pass" report$n_blockers_failed # count of failed blocker items #> [1] 0  # Per-item detail (checklist order preserved): report$items[[1]]$id      # "falsifiability" #> [1] "falsifiability" report$items[[1]]$status  # "pass" / "warn" / "fail" #> [1] "pass" report$items[[1]]$score   # numeric in [0, 1] #> [1] 1 cat(tf_report(theory, format = "json")) #> { #>   "theory_id": "panic-network", #>   "schema_version": "1.0", #>   "maturity": "building", #>   "aggregate_score": 77.6, #>   "gate": "pass", #>   "n_blockers_failed": 0, #>   "items": [ #>     { #>       "id": "falsifiability", #>       "status": "pass", #>       "score": 1, #>       "weight": 0.15, #>       "severity_if_fail": "blocker", #>       "citation": "Popper (1959); Bacharach (1989)" #>     }, #>     { #>       "id": "precision", #>       "status": "pass", #>       "score": 1, #>       "weight": 0.1, #>       "severity_if_fail": "warning", #>       "citation": "Meehl (1967, 1990)" #>     }, #>     { #>       "id": "risk_severity", #>       "status": "warn", #>       "score": 0, #>       "weight": 0.1, #>       "severity_if_fail": "warning", #>       "citation": "Mayo (2018); Meehl (1990)" #>     }, #>     { #>       "id": "parsimony", #>       "status": "pass", #>       "score": 1, #>       "weight": 0.08, #>       "severity_if_fail": "warning", #>       "citation": "Forster & Sober (1994); Lakatos (1970)" #>     }, #>     { #>       "id": "non_redundancy", #>       "status": "pass", #>       "score": 0.857, #>       "weight": 0.1, #>       "severity_if_fail": "warning", #>       "citation": "Kelley (1927); Le et al. title: Building, checking and diagramming a theory

title: Systematic Theory Development
text: See Get started fuller walk-building, checking diagramming theory.
code: library(theoryforge)  # Read an existing theory (or build one incrementally with tf_theory + tf_add_*) theory <- tf_read("fixtures/panic-network.theory.yaml") tf_validate(theory)  # Score it against the 12-item rigour checklist report <- tf_check(theory) report$aggregate_score   # 84.8 report$gate              # "pass"  # Operationalised severity and a preregistration document tf_severity(theory) cat(tf_preregister(theory))  # Diagram the structure (byte-identical to the Python output) cat(tf_diagram(theory, type = "nomological_net"))  # DEVELOP: appraise an amendment as progressive or degenerating v2 <- tf_read("fixtures/panic-network-2026-v2.theory.yaml") tf_appraise_amendment(v2, theory)$verdict   # "progressive"  # LITERATURE: map a corpus and position the theory against it corpus <- tf_read_corpus("fixtures/panic-corpus.yaml") landscape <- tf_landscape(theory, corpus) landscape$under_theorised_fronts   # themes no theory addresses landscape$redundancy_risk          # crowded themes

text: every unordered pair constructs, embeds definition supplied embedder computes cosine similarity (rounded 6 decimals). Returns data frame sorted descending cosine (, b), flagging pairs threshold review. assistive screen complements deterministic lexical tf_redundancy_check() parity-exempt. See API_SPEC.md section 23.

title: Embedding-based pairwise construct-redundancy screen — tf_embedding_redundancy
code: theory <- tf_theory("demo-1", "A demonstration theory") |>   tf_add_construct("c_arousal", "Arousal", "bodily activation") |>   tf_add_construct("c_threat", "Threat", "appraised danger") # A toy deterministic embedder: bag-of-words counts over a fixed vocabulary. vocab <- c("bodily", "activation", "appraised", "danger") embedder <- function(def) {   words <- strsplit(tolower(def), "\\s+")[[1]]   vapply(vocab, function(w) sum(words == w), numeric(1)) } tf_embedding_redundancy(theory, embedder) #>           a        b cosine flag #> 1 c_arousal c_threat      0   ok

title: theoryforge 0.1.0
text: First public release. package provides reproducible workflow building, developing testing scientific theories, behaviour pinned shared specification (API_SPEC.md) R Python twins return identical verdicts byte-identical diagram intermediate representations. Core: theory-object input, output structural validation; 12-item rigour checklist weighted aggregate score blocker gate; diagram intermediate representations (nomological net, provenance, causal DAG); deterministic lexical construct-redundancy screen. Workflow modes: builder API auto-logged provenance (BUILDING); operationalised severity rubric preregistration export (TESTING); Lakatosian progressive-versus-degenerating amendment appraisal (DEVELOPMENT). Literature layer: deterministic bibliometric mapping (tf_litmap, tf_landscape, tf_lit_diagram) parity-exempt OpenAlex corpus adapter. Testing review: lavaan model-syntax compilation (tf_compile_sem) reviewer-facing audit dossier (tf_dossier). Simulation, reporting deposit: deterministic dynamical-system runner (tf_simulate), Quarto report wrapper (tf_render_report), opt-embedding redundancy screen (tf_embedding_redundancy), OSF deposit adapter (tf_osf_push, dry-run default).
